repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1 Support Proposition 1: null-annihilator criterion for proximal ATE point identification

published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 and BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023: linear functionals can be identified despite nonunique or weakly identified nuisance bridges

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: native proximal threshold/interval taxonomy and null-annihilator support result; this proposal retargets it to a long-term two-sample loading

published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 + GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 + BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023 cover nonunique bridges, proxy partial ID, and strong functionals, but not this exact long-term alpha_LT envelope

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: prior scalar singular-value/native-selector threshold angle; this proposal changes to local weak-completeness for the long-term loading

published_prior_art.cite: BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023 and KallusMaoUehara2021: weakly identified functionals and minimax negative-control bridge learning, but not the proposed three-regime long-term alpha_LT phase diagram

structure_flags.label: Introduction
structure_flags.one_line: §4 has five prose paragraphs rather than six distinct required sub-paragraphs; implications for the community and how research benefits are merged.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The null-annihilator criterion duplicates eid_proximal_phase_v1 Support Proposition 1; keep it only as a support proposition, not a novelty-bearing theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The linear-functional/nonunique-bridge kernel is already covered by ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 and BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship comparison cites MiaoGengTchetgen2018, GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023, and ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 without a §/equation/page/open-problem anchor tying them to the claimed alpha_LT gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The generic open finite-support frontier has only an abstract K_rho,c,m matrix example, not a concrete proximal negative-control observed law with endpoint realization.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The weak-completeness flagship cites BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023, CarrascoFlorensRenault2007, and ChenReiss2011 without location-level anchors for the specific local phase-diagram gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The drafter checklist supplied only BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023 and KallusMaoUehara2021 as Conjecture 2 comparators, omitting the inverse-problem papers the proposal itself relies on.

soundness_flags.label: Locked parameters
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked identifying assumptions include alpha_LT(P)=0, but the flagship boundary studies alpha_LT(P)>0; the lock should define alpha_LT as the object, not assume the point-ID side.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The first-order half-width formula uses the arbitrary slack constant epsilon_P; sharp width depends on the support function/coordinate slacks of the bridge fiber, not generally epsilon_P times ||Pi_N c||.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption open-weak says omega_n is 'bounded relative to' named constants while Conjecture 2 requires omega_n -> infinity, finite tau, and 0; the allowed local sequence class is not well-defined.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the r_LT formula by an independently defined support-function radius for the bounded bridge fiber, then make alpha_LT=0 the point-ID criterion and the radius the interval-width object.
Add a concrete finite proximal law witness with named cell probabilities for (A,W,Z,S,X,Y^L), not just K_rho,c,m arrays, and show the bridge-envelope endpoints are realizable in that law.

Add section/equation/page anchors for each flagship comparator, especially ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023, GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023, BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023, CarrascoFlorensRenault2007, and ChenReiss2011.
Rewrite the locked block so alpha_LT(P)=0 is the tested frontier condition rather than an identifying assumption, and separate the Introduction into the six required subparagraphs.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal around a support-function width object plus a real finite-law witness; without that, the kernel remains field-tier and below the flagship floor.
